compiled_state.author: Claude

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — security (reference overlay)
- Source: Anthropic claude-code-security-review (MIT) — 10-category vuln taxonomy + exploit_scenario + confidence gate (>0.8). Attribute MIT.
- Triggers: auth/authz/crypto/session paths; files touching secrets, input handling, deserialization, SQL/command construction, network/CORS config (reuse catalog applies_to globs).
- Scanner pre-pass: optional Semgrep/Gitleaks/Trivy-secrets as ground truth (LLM-as-judge over findings).
- Base-escalation: any security-shaped concern even without a trigger match.
- Severity: hard auto-block reserved for High/Critical + secrets; else advisory.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — infrastructure / IaC
- Source (Apache-2.0): KICS 14-category taxonomy + Checkov categories + kube-linter + Trivy severity; AWS Well-Architected pillar names (paraphrase). Author rubric in own words.
- Dimensions: IAM/least-privilege, network exposure, encryption at-rest/in-transit, secrets, insecure defaults, container/k8s hardening, reliability/resource governance, logging/observability, CI/CD supply-chain (pin action SHAs), cost (advisory), structure/validity.
- Triggers: *.tf,*.tfvars,.terraform.lock.hcl; Dockerfile/Containerfile/compose; k8s/helm/kustomize (content-gate *.yaml on apiVersion+kind); CFN/SAM; *.bicep/ARM; *.rego; .github/workflows/*, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile.
- Scanner pre-pass: Checkov/Trivy as deterministic ground truth; overlay reasons/prioritizes (CodeRabbit pattern).
- Base-escalation: provider/resource/secret literal in a non-IaC diff.
- LICENSE FLAG: avoid CIS Benchmarks (CC-BY-NC-SA) — Apache scanners map to CIS. Terrascan archived / Datree EOL.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — testing
- Source: PR-Agent relevant_tests (Apache-2.0), PyNose 15-smell taxonomy (Apache-2.0), Danger code-changed-without-tests (MIT), mutation tools (Stryker/PIT Apache, mutmut BSD); FIRST/AAA/test-pyramid (free).
- Dimensions: test presence/adequacy for CHANGED behavior; right level (pyramid); edge/error/negative cases; AAA, one-assert, naming, DAMP>DRY, behavior-not-implementation; isolation/mocking (no over-mock); FIRST determinism/speed (no sleep/flaky); assertion quality; test smells; maintainability.
- Triggers: *_test.*, *.spec.*, __tests__/, tests/, src/test/**, _test.go.
- HIGH-VALUE deterministic trigger: production code changed WITHOUT a corresponding test change (Danger pattern); + diff-coverage gate if coverage data present.
- Base-escalation: base flags risky logic/error-handling -> assess test adequacy.
- LICENSE FLAG: don't vendor tsDetect code (GPL) — use PyNose taxonomy (Apache). Google SWE book CC-BY-ND (cite only).

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — documentation
- Source: Google dev-docs style guide (CC-BY-4.0) + Microsoft Writing Style Guide (CC-BY-4.0) + Diataxis (doc-type taxonomy) + Vale/proselint(BSD)/markdownlint(MIT) + PEP257/TSDoc + Keep a Changelog; CodeRabbit docstring-coverage model.
- Dimensions (two halves): A) prose/docs quality — Diataxis type-fit, voice/grammar, mechanics/consistency, clarity, inclusive language, structure, examples, accessibility, correctness/freshness; B) code-is-documented — docstring presence/completeness on public symbols, format conformance, public-API doc coverage, README adequacy, changelog entry, comment quality.
- Triggers: *.md/*.rst/docs/, README*/CHANGELOG*, docstring-heavy diffs, net-new exported symbols.
- HIGH-VALUE trigger: public/exported symbol changed WITHOUT doc update (AST detect + no doc file touched).
- Scanner pre-pass: Vale/markdownlint/proselint for mechanics; reserve LLM for semantic (type-fit, freshness, audience-adequacy).
- LICENSE FLAG: watch CC-BY-SA on some Vale packages; ship ATTRIBUTIONS note for CC-BY (Google/MS) sources.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — performance
- Source: Sentry performance-issue detector taxonomy (N+1 DB/API, slow query >500ms, large payload, main-thread I/O, render-blocking) = concepts; perflint(MIT)/Clippy(MIT-Apache)/PMD(BSD)/SpotBugs(LGPL taxonomy-ref); Google eng-practices complexity/over-engineering (CC-BY); Core Web Vitals; eslint-plugin-react-perf (MIT).
- Dimensions: algorithmic/complexity regressions; data-access/IO amplification (N+1, chatty network, slow/unbounded query, blocking-IO-on-async) [highest signal]; allocations/copies; inefficient data structures; caching/memoization; unbounded growth/memory; concurrency appropriateness; frontend render budgets.
- Triggers: DB/ORM/query-builder paths; loop-containing-a-call (AST = N+1); async/threadpool introductions; serialization/large-data; CURATED hot-path allowlist; frontend files.
- FLAG (WEAK TRIGGERS — escalation-dependent): globs detect file-touches-DB, not introduced-N+1; big-O/memoization/unbounded-growth/premature-concurrency have NO reliable file/AST signature. Most base-escalation-reliant overlay. Two-tier: deterministic triggers = recall net for N+1/blocking-IO/large-payload/render-block; base-escalation for semantic dimensions.
- LICENSE FLAG: don't vendor Semgrep registry rules (restrictive) — use perflint/Clippy/PMD.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — LLM prompts / skills
- Source: Anthropic prompting + skill-authoring best practices (PROPRIETARY — paraphrase only) + OWASP LLM Top 10 / Prompt-Injection Cheat Sheet (CC-BY-SA — paraphrase to avoid share-alike) + permissive tool taxonomies: promptfoo (MIT) red-team plugins, garak (Apache) probes, DSPy signatures (MIT), Inspect AI (MIT).
- Dimensions (two halves): A) prompt/skill quality — clarity, structure/delimiting, role framing, examples/few-shot, output-format/schema, grounding, context-justification/conciseness, skill discovery metadata, appropriate degrees of freedom, consistency/durability, workflows/feedback, tool/function-def quality, eval-spec quality, calibrated emphasis (avoid over-prompting); B) safety/injection — instruction hierarchy, segregate untrusted content, least-privilege tool scoping, HITL for high-impact, system-prompt/secret hygiene, output validation, obfuscation resistance, jailbreak/override resistance, refusal/abstention, access-control awareness (RBAC/BOLA/BFLA), adversarial test coverage.
- Triggers: *.prompt/prompts/, SKILL.md/.claude/skills//.claude/agents//*.agent.md, .cursor/rules/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/copilot-instructions, reviewers//system_prompt constants, tool-def schemas, eval specs (*.eval.*, promptfooconfig, Inspect tasks, DSPy signatures); content signals (SYSTEM_PROMPT constants, name+description frontmatter, function-schema markers).
- LICENSE FLAG: best sources proprietary (Anthropic/OpenAI) -> paraphrase; OWASP CC-BY-SA -> paraphrase; permissive tool taxonomies usable with notice. Author rubric in own words.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — API compatibility / backwards-compat
- Source (all permissive): buf breaking (Apache; proto tiered WIRE/WIRE_JSON/PACKAGE/FILE), oasdiff (Apache; OpenAPI), graphql-js findSchemaChanges (MIT; Breaking/Dangerous/Non-breaking), Griffe (ISC; Python), cargo-semver-checks (Apache/MIT; Rust), japicmp/revapi (Apache; Java), api-extractor (MIT; TS); standards SemVer/Conventional-Commits/Google AIP-180/181/185.
- Dimensions: A) symbol existence (removal/rename); B) signature/shape (param removed/reordered/kind; NEW REQUIRED param=silent breaker; type change; cardinality); C) return/output contract (narrowed return; removed response property/status); D) enum (removed=breaking; ADDED=dangerous); E) wire/serialization tier (severity modifier); F) validation/semantics tightening (changed default=dangerous; SAME-SIGNATURE-CHANGED-BEHAVIOR = LLM-only); G) deprecation/versioning correctness (MAJOR taken? Conventional-Commits flagged? deprecation-before-removal? reserve-not-reuse).
- Severity vocab: adopt BREAKING/DANGEROUS/NON_BREAKING/INFO (graphql-js) + ERR/WARN (oasdiff); DANGEROUS/WARN routes to the LLM verifier.
- Triggers: *.proto/buf.yaml; openapi*/swagger*; *.graphql/*.gql; version-field bumps; api-extractor *.api.md; *.pyi/*.d.ts/__all__. Semantic: exported-symbol diff (preferred).
- Base-escalation CRITICAL for: same-signature-changed-behavior, hand-rolled HTTP/RPC w/o schema artifact, config-as-API.
- LICENSE: all permissive, no blockers. FLAG: no LLM rubric exists -> author original; tools miss some dims (Griffe return-type, cargo-semver-checks signatures) -> LLM/escalation.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — dependencies / supply-chain
- Source (Apache/MIT): OpenSSF Scorecard (19 checks=best taxonomy), Google OSV-Scanner, OWASP Dependency-Check, Trivy/Grype (EPSS+KEV severity), pip-audit, GitHub dependency-review-action (MIT; closest PR-time rubric); SLSA + SPDX.
- 7 dimensions: known vulns (CVE/OSV; CVSS+EPSS+KEV); malicious/typosquat/slopsquat (install scripts/obfuscation/behavioral — separate LLM check, audit misses novel); license compatibility (SPDX + copyleft intrusion); pinning/lockfile hygiene (hash pinning; manifest<->lockfile consistency); maintenance health (Scorecard); minimal/justified additions; integrity/provenance (signed releases, SBOM, SLSA).
- Triggers: ecosystem manifest+lockfile globs (package.json/lock, requirements/poetry.lock/uv.lock, go.mod/sum, Cargo.toml/lock, Gemfile/lock, pom.xml/build.gradle, composer, *.csproj).
- Deterministic escalation: manifest changed WITHOUT matching lockfile change.
- Scanner pre-pass: OSV-Scanner/Scorecard/Grype as ground truth; LLM-judge over findings + typosquat/justification dims.
- LICENSE FLAG: Snyk proprietary (concept-only); Socket product-docs (concepts not text); ScanCode LicenseDB CC-BY-4.0 (attribution IF embedded — avoid via public-domain SPDX ids). Not blocked.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — database / migrations / SQL
- Source: strong_migrations (MIT; unsafe-op->safe-rewrite), Atlas lint analyzers (Apache; 50+ DB-aware), django-migration-linter (Apache; reversibility), sqlfluff (MIT; STYLE only not safety); CodeRabbit migration checks = behavior benchmark.
- 7 dimensions: D1 destructive/irreversible (DROP; rename-as-destructive; lossy type change); D2 locking/zero-downtime (non-CONCURRENTLY index; NOT NULL+default rewrite; validating constraint scan -> NOT VALID then VALIDATE); D3 index correctness/query perf; D4 backfill/data-migration safety (batched; fail-on-existing-rows); D5 reversibility (down-migrations; RunSQL/RunPython reverse); D6 transaction safety (CONCURRENTLY-in-txn illegal; lock/statement timeout); D7 backward-compat/expand-contract. Optional D8 type hygiene.
- Triggers: migrations/migrate/alembic/db-migrate/Flyway/Liquibase/prisma-migrations/drizzle/goose globs + *.sql (scope-gated) + schema files.
- HIGH-VALUE escalation: ORM model changed but NO migration file present (missing-migration).
- LICENSE FLAG: Squawk packages GPL-3.0 -> factual cross-check only; source wording from strong_migrations/Atlas/django-migration-linter (permissive equivalents exist). pt-osc GPL-2.0 (rationale only). Not blocked.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — accessibility (a11y)  [PROJECT-DEPENDENT]
- Source: WCAG 2.2 AA (W3C license; authoritative criteria) + eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y (MIT; directly reusable rule set) + axe-core rule TAXONOMY (MPL — reuse concepts only, do NOT vendor source) + WAI-ARIA APG + ACT Rules (W3C). No turnkey LLM rubric (Microsoft a11y-llm-eval / CodeA11y = prompt-design refs only); author from the linter taxonomy validated vs WCAG 2.2 AA.
- Dimensions: D1 text alternatives (alt); D2 forms/labels; D3 color/contrast (>=4.5:1; verify-rendered flag); D4 keyboard/focus (+ WCAG 2.2 target-size>=24px, dragging-alternative, focus-not-obscured); D5 ARIA correctness; D6 name/role/value (vs APG patterns); D7 semantic HTML; D8 headings/landmarks; D9 lang/document; D10 media/motion. Impact tiers: critical/serious/moderate/minor.
- Triggers: *.jsx/tsx/vue/svelte/astro/html + templates (erb/hbs/blade/twig/liquid/razor/cshtml) + css/scss + {components,ui,views,pages,layouts}/ dirs + *.stories.*; content signals (JSX, role=/aria-*, onClick, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, .focus()).
- Base-escalation: interactive element / ARIA / tabindex / focus mgmt / contrast literal / custom widget in ANY file.
- RELEVANCE FLAG: project-dependent — HIGH for frontend/UI, LOW-TO-ZERO for backend/CLI/library/infra (incl. rebar itself). Gate on UI globs + a repo-level "frontend present?" heuristic; keep dormant on non-UI repos to avoid noise.
- CEILING FLAG: rendered contrast, focus visibility, screen-reader announcement order, decorative-vs-meaningful alt are NOT statically verifiable -> emit as "manual verification required" (pass-2 "insufficient" verdict), never pass/fail.
- LICENSE: use jsx-a11y (MIT) + W3C criteria; do NOT vendor axe-core (MPL) / pa11y HTMLCS (LGPL) source; WAVE proprietary -> avoid.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — internationalization / localization (i18n/l10n)  [PROJECT-DEPENDENT]
- Source: eslint-plugin-formatjs rule taxonomy (MIT; ~18 ICU/message defects = rubric backbone) + eslint-plugin-i18next (MIT; hardcoded-string detection) + Unicode CLDR / ICU MessageFormat (Unicode-3.0, permissive; plural categories + locale formatting) + W3C i18n best practices (W3C license). No turnkey LLM rubric; author from these.
- Dimensions: A) externalization (no hardcoded user-facing strings; route via t()/FormattedMessage; NO sentence concatenation); B) ICU correctness (plural/selectordinal w/ CLDR categories; other-branch present; valid ICU; placeholders supplied; avoid nested/multi-plural); C) message metadata/translator context (stable id; description/translator comment; no emoji); D) locale-aware formatting (Intl.DateTimeFormat/NumberFormat; currency; locale-aware collation/case — no naive toLowerCase); E) bidi/RTL (logical start/end not left/right; text-expansion tolerance; bidi-safe interpolation); F) encoding (UTF-8; normalization; no 1char=1byte); G) language tagging (BCP-47; lang/dir).
- Triggers: locale files (*.po/*.pot/*.arb/*.xliff/*.xlf; messages*.properties; {locales,i18n,lang,translations}/**.{json,yaml,yml}; *.strings; values*/strings.xml; *.ftl path-gated) + translation call-site signals (t(), FormattedMessage, gettext, ngettext, $t, trans()) when an i18n lib is imported.
- Activation rule: (locale-glob match) OR (i18n-lib import present) OR (explicit base-escalation). Do NOT fire on a lone string literal.
- Base-escalation: user-facing string changes / hardcoded literals / sentence concatenation in a project WITHOUT established i18n.
- RELEVANCE FLAG: project-dependent — HIGH for user-facing apps, LOW/NEAR-ZERO for backend/CLI/library with no user-facing strings (incl. rebar itself). Suppress on non-UI repos.
- LICENSE: formatjs/i18next/i18n-ally MIT, CLDR/ICU Unicode-3.0, W3C permissive — all usable. FLAG: GNU gettext MANUAL is GFDL (copyleft) -> restate conventions, don't copy text; vendor blog rubrics proprietary -> orientation only.

comments.author: Claude
comments.body:
DESIGN DECISION — APPROVED: base->overlay escalation mechanism (reviewer-selection layer)

Overlay activation = UNION of two sources:
  overlays_to_run = { overlays whose deterministic triggers (applies_to globs / content signatures) match the diff }
                  UNION { overlays the base reviewer escalated to }

Mechanism:
1. Round A (parallel): the BASE reviewer ALWAYS runs (every change) alongside deterministically-triggered overlays. The base emits its pass-1 findings AND a bounded escalation signal: recommend_overlays: [{overlay_id, reason}], where overlay_id is constrained to the FIXED catalog enum (base cannot invent an overlay; one-line concrete reason required, same abstention discipline as findings).
2. A DETERMINISTIC orchestrator takes the union and dispatches any escalated overlays not already run in Round A.
3. Round B (parallel): those escalated overlays run their pass-1 evidence contract.
4. ALL findings (base + Round-A + Round-B overlays) flow into the SAME pass-2 binary-verifier + pass-3 deterministic typed gate.

Safety / why this does not reintroduce "the LLM decides":
- Escalation only decides WHICH advisory reviewers run; it cannot affect the verdict. A false escalation costs one extra reviewer pass (not a wrong gate); a missed escalation is a recall gap backstopped by the deterministic trigger union; an overlay's findings still pass through pass-2 verification + pass-3 deterministic gate, so escalation can never bypass the block logic or force a finding through. Escalation lives on the recall/selection side (model-influence acceptable); the precision/decision side stays deterministic.

Bounds:
- ONE HOP, no recursion: base escalates to overlays; overlays do NOT re-escalate (prevents specialist->specialist cascades).
- Per-change CAP on number of overlays dispatched (e.g. top-N by base's stated confidence) so a noisy diff doesn't fan out to all overlays.
- Conservative by prompt: escalate only with a concrete reason.

Where it lives: extends the existing reviewer-selection seam (select_code_reviewers in code_review.py) into two stages — Stage 1 deterministic (base + glob/content-matched overlays); Stage 2 escalation (after base returns, union in base's recommend_overlays, dispatch any not already run). Per-overlay escalation cues are recorded in the OVERLAY CATALOG comments on this epic (e.g. performance "introduced-N+1 has no signature", api-compat "same-signature behavior change", db "ORM model changed without a migration", a11y "interactive/ARIA/focus in any file", i18n "hardcoded user-facing strings without i18n"). Performance is the most escalation-reliant overlay (weakest deterministic triggers).

Status: APPROVED / locked. To be reflected as a reviewer-selection acceptance criterion when E2 is built. (No implementation yet.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PREREQUISITE REALIGNMENT — reflect recent codebase changes (2026-06). Same process as the plan-review (5fd2) + code-review-capability (b744) epics: inventoried the shipped foundations against this framework epic and corrected the description (new '## Prerequisite foundations' section + AC2/AC6 edits). Closed prerequisite epics NOT reopened.

KEY SHIFT — the reviewer migration is PARTLY done:
- da27 (closed) already migrated the existing reviewers (code_quality/security/tests/spec_alignment/ticket_quality/completion_verifier) to the front-matter CONTRACT model (inputs/outputs/execution_mode/dimension/applies_to + derived index + CI drift gate). That is the PROMPT-STORAGE half of 'migrate existing reviewers'.
- BUT they still emit the OLD pass-1 shape {findings:[{severity,dimension,detail,citations}],summary}, NOT this epic's pass-1 EVIDENCE contract {finding,criteria[],evidence[],scenarios[],impact}. So AC6 is now the pass-1-SHAPE + Pass-2/Pass-3 migration LAYERED on the already-contracted prompts, not a from-scratch rewrite.

OTHER SUBSTRATE NOW CONCRETE:
- findings.py (normalize_finding) + aggregate.py exist; review_code is SINGLE-PASS today (no Pass-2 verifier, no Pass-3 gate — both still NEW WORK here).
- Runner = PydanticAIRunner (e6d9; LangGraph cut over), honoring execution_mode; workflow engine + Inspect-AI eval seam shipped.
- Pass-2 RE-GROUNDING + the cited-reference VETO now have a substrate: the 8f6c grounding oracle (refute_absence/applies/scan). The overlay catalogs' 'Scanner pre-pass (Semgrep/Gitleaks/Trivy)' maps to the oracle's extensible detector registry (OpenGrep/ast-grep shipped); security/secrets/CVE detectors still pending (shared with b744).

This epic remains the shared three-pass FRAMEWORK that both plan-review (5fd2, adopts from here) and code-review (b744, depends_on here) build on.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 COACH (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety) — VERDICT: NOT-APPLICABLE (no findings)

T11 is an overlay that fires only when the plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills existing data. This Pass-4 coach plan does neither:
- It is an ADDITIVE, in-pipeline LLM computation that runs AFTER Pass-3 over the surviving findings and emits advisory coaching ([{move_id, subject, finding_refs}]) shipped in the SAME result.
- It explicitly does NOT own any persisted artifact: the stable per-finding `id` is MINTED by the orchestrator (ca03) and the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is owned by db7b — this coach is a stated CONSUMER ("findings + coaching ship in ONE result so the reference resolves locally (no circular alignment)"), so there is no new persisted-format definition, no in-place rewrite of existing data, and no backfill of historical reviews.
- No DDL, no large-table migration, no dual-write window, no backfill batching — none of the T11 binary checks (online/expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write, rollback/data-loss) have a target here.

ANTI-FP applies: this is a non-persisted, per-review computation. T11 PASS not-applicable. (Note: if a later revision were to add a NEW persisted/replayed schema owned by THIS ticket — rather than consuming ca03/db7b's — T11 would re-trigger; that ownership boundary is currently clean.)

affirmations: T11 (not-applicable — no schema/persisted-format change and no data backfill in scope).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pass-1 (T8 overlay) review note: structural-completeness probe on the Pass-4 coach move registry / subject schema. Verified against src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (MOVE_REGISTRY + load_move_registry) and passes.py (_pass4_model, pass4_coach, _validate_subject) — move_id vocabulary, subject validator, and the no-valid-subject fallback are all fully specified and backed by code. One PROVEN gap: the plan declares each move carries `triggers (criteria/finding-class)` and that moves 11/12 fire conditionally ("when the ticket has children" / "per review"), but the registry schema (`{id, name, template}`) defines no `triggers` field and selection is delegated wholly to the LLM with no co-located decision rule. See checklist finding below.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "superseded by vivid-gang-day (3316-252f-e945-4521): four-pass kernel-extraction scope; reviewer-migration dropped (code_review.py is throwaway, real code review is b744)". Session: b115faba.

compiled_state.description:
# Realign rebar reviews to the three-pass (evidence -> binary-verify -> deterministic-gate) structure

## Context
Industry practice and 2025-2026 research converge: an LLM reviewer should surface cited evidence against a locked rubric; a SEPARATE agent should validate each finding via atomic binary sub-questions; and a DETERMINISTIC policy (not the model) should decide severity and blocking. LLMs are unreliable at holistic severity/confidence judgments, so neither is emitted by the model. This epic builds that shared three-pass framework and migrates rebar's existing reviewers onto it.

## Prerequisite foundations — current codebase state (2026-06)

Foundations shipped since this epic was written; the design is UNCHANGED, but the substrate is now CONCRETE for
parts and the reviewer migration is PARTLY done (the closed prerequisite epics are NOT reopened — this realigns
THIS epic to what they delivered):

- **Reviewer PROMPT migration DONE; three-pass STRUCTURE migration still pending (epic `da27`, closed).** The
  existing reviewers (`code_quality`/`security`/`tests`/`spec_alignment`/`ticket_quality`/`completion_verifier`)
  were migrated to the front-matter CONTRACT model — `inputs`/`outputs` schema-name contracts, `execution_mode`,
  `dimension`, `applies_to` globs, a derived index + CI drift gate (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`, `reviewers/*.md`).
  That is the PROMPT-STORAGE half of 'migrate existing reviewers'. They still emit the OLD pass-1 shape
  `{findings:[{severity, dimension, detail, citations}], summary}`, NOT this epic's pass-1 EVIDENCE contract
  `{finding, criteria[], evidence[], scenarios[], impact}` — migrating the pass-1 SHAPE + adding Pass-2/Pass-3 is
  THIS epic's remaining work, layered on the already-contracted prompts.
- **Pass-1 data substrate (partial) + single-pass `review_code`.** `src/rebar/llm/findings.py`
  (`normalize_finding`, citation coercion, schema validation) + `src/rebar/llm/aggregate.py` (cluster +
  severity×agreement rank) exist; `review_code` (`src/rebar/llm/code_review.py`) runs reviewers + aggregates today
  as a SINGLE PASS. There is NO Pass-2 verifier agent and NO Pass-3 deterministic gate yet — both are NEW WORK here.
- **Provider-agnostic runner + workflow engine (epic `e6d9`, closed).** `PydanticAIRunner` (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py`)
  is the sole runtime (LangGraph cut over), honoring the `execution_mode` {single_turn|agentic} seam — the substrate
  Pass-1 and the separate-context Pass-2 verifier run on; the declarative workflow engine (`run_workflow`) + the
  Inspect-AI eval seam shipped alongside.
- **Code-grounding for Pass-2 (epic `8f6c`, closed).** `src/rebar/grounding/` (the `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan`
  facade, exported from `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py`) is the substrate for Pass-2's RE-GROUNDING in the actual
  code + the cited-code-reference-accuracy VETO. The overlay catalogs' 'Scanner pre-pass (Semgrep/Gitleaks/Trivy)'
  maps to the oracle's EXTENSIBLE detector registry (`detectors/registry.py`; OpenGrep/ast-grep shipped) — but
  security/secrets/CVE detectors are NOT yet shipped (only `smell_generic` + applicability), so those remain new
  work (here or shared with the code-review capability epic `b744`).

## Success Criteria
- All rebar reviews emit the pass-1 evidence contract; a shared pass-2 verifier agent and pass-3 deterministic aggregator exist and are used.
- Severity is computed deterministically from structured attributes; validity/confidence from typed binary sub-answers. No model-emitted severity number, confidence number, or high/med/low rating in the decision path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass-1 finding contract: `{finding (free text), criteria[] (each in the prompt's locked rubric set), evidence[] (flexible free text: code citations, prose, OR absence rationale), scenarios[] (free text), impact (free text)}`. Criteria is an array. No model severity/confidence.
- [ ] Pass-2 verifier = a separate agent / independent context that re-grounds in the actual code (via the shipped 8f6c grounding-oracle facade `refute_absence`/`scan` for cited-reference + smell grounding) and emits per finding: (a) severity attributes `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` (coarse ordinals); (b) typed binary sub-answers `{verdict: yes|no|insufficient, evidence_span/reason}`.
- [ ] Binary sub-question set (typed): the ONLY veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a code citation is present (no-op for absence findings / non-code reviews); all others GRADED: is-verifiable (signal), evidence-entails-finding, path-reachable, impact-follows-necessarily, no-viable-alternative-explanation, no-existing-mitigation, severity-claim-justified.
- [ ] Pass-3 deterministic aggregator: conditional veto gate -> graded confidence (weighted fraction / log-odds) -> severity-from-attributes -> `block | advisory` against per-criteria thresholds (project-overridable; defaults start high -> almost all findings advisory while calibration data is gathered). No interactive escalation path (autonomous).
- [ ] Verifier prompt obeys the research rules: atomicity (one proposition per question); independence (do not show the finding's own conclusion when answering each sub-question); verdict-with-citation, NOT verdict-with-proposed-fix; allow "insufficient".
- [ ] Existing code-review reviewers (review_ticket and the code-quality/security/tests reviewers) migrated to the pass-1 EVIDENCE contract + shared pass-2/pass-3 — these are ALREADY on the da27 front-matter CONTRACT model (inputs/outputs/execution_mode/dimension/applies_to + derived index), so this is the pass-1-SHAPE + Pass-2/Pass-3 migration ON TOP of that, NOT a from-scratch reviewer rewrite; findings schema extended (`src/rebar/llm/findings.py`); aggregation + citation-resolution + schema-pin tests updated. (completion_verifier is OUT OF SCOPE: not migrated, unchanged.)
- [ ] Rubric sourced from Google eng-practices + Anthropic security taxonomy (locked + versioned); Conventional Comments not adopted.
- [ ] Known limitations recorded: veto-brittleness mitigated by the single conditional veto; decomposition controls validity PRECISION not finding-type RECALL; threshold calibration is explicitly out of scope (separate future work).
compiled_state.title: Realign rebar reviews to three-pass (evidence -> binary-verify -> deterministic gate); migrate existing reviewers
